content: I'm trying to decide between a new laptop for my blockchain work. I need something that can handle smart contract development and running local testnets without overheating.
What kind of development environment are you running? Are you using Truffle, Hardhat, or something else?
I use Hardhat mostly, plus I need to run multiple Docker containers for different DeFi protocols I'm testing. My current MacBook Pro from 2018 is struggling.
How much RAM do you have? And are you looking for something portable or would you consider a desktop setup?

I have 16GB and I definitely need portability. I'm running between client meetings and my home office where I'm caring for my parents. The laptop needs to last through marathon training sessions too - I'm training for the Portland Marathon next month.
Ah, so battery life is important. Have you considered the new M3 MacBook Pro or maybe a high-end Windows laptop with good thermals?

I was looking at the M3 but I'm worried about compatibility with some of the DeFi tools I use. Plus I need to run some Windows-specific software for my DJ gigs on weekends. I DJ at local events around Portland.
A dual-boot setup or virtualization could work. What's your budget range? And do you need a dedicated GPU for any of your work?

I can go up to $2500. I don't need a dedicated GPU for work, but I do use one for my leatherworking hobby - I design patterns in 3D software before cutting. My parents are in their 80s, so I need to be able to work from their house when they need help.

That helps narrow it down. The Framework laptop with a DIY upgrade path might be good for your needs - you can customize RAM and storage, and it's repairable if you're into that kind of thing with your leatherworking mindset.
Interesting, I hadn't considered Framework. How's the Linux compatibility? I sometimes spin up Linux VMs for testing different blockchain networks.
It's excellent for Linux. You can even get it pre-installed. The main trade-off is it's not as polished as a MacBook, but it's very flexible for your development workflow.

That flexibility sounds perfect. I could use different OS setups for different DeFi projects. Last month I was working on a yield farming protocol that needed specific node versions I couldn't get on my Mac.
Exactly. And since you're in Portland, there's a good tech community there if you need support. Have you thought about what screen size you prefer?
I was thinking 14 or 16 inches. I need enough screen real estate for multiple terminals when I'm debugging smart contracts, but I don't want it too bulky for carrying to my parents' house.
The 16-inch would give you more workspace for your development environment. Plus it has better cooling for those long testnet runs.
You're right. I'll probably go with the 16-inch Framework. I can upgrade the RAM to 32GB later if I need it. My current setup crashes when I have too many browser tabs open with blockchain explorers.
32GB is a smart choice for your workflow. The Framework should handle everything you throw at it - from DeFi development to your DJ software to those 3D leatherworking designs.
Perfect. I'll order it this week. I need it before my parents' anniversary party - I'm making them leather wallets as gifts, and I want to finish the designs on the new machine.
That's a thoughtful gift. The new laptop will definitely speed up your design process. Let me know if you need help with the setup when it arrives.
Will do. I'm also thinking about setting up a home automation system to help care for my parents - motion sensors and automated lights so they don't have to fumble in the dark.
That's a great idea. There are some excellent systems that integrate with voice assistants. Would you like recommendations for something user-friendly for older adults?
Yes, please. Something simple they can control with voice commands. My mom struggles with small buttons but loves talking to Alexa.
I'd suggest looking at Amazon's Astro robot or a comprehensive Echo system with smart plugs and motion sensors. It could really help with their independence while giving you peace of mind.
Thanks, I'll check those out. The new laptop should arrive just in time for me to set everything up before my marathon training really ramps up next month.
